relatedness_reasoning: Both files concern flagged/at-risk accounts with overlapping customer and account identifiers; Risk and Compliance consumes arrears flags and produces transaction hold notices as a downstream compliance action, forming a logical pipeline.

mappings.reasoning: System-generated unique identifier for the hold notice; no source in inbound schema.
Both are ISO8601 timestamps; outbound timestamp likely derived from or correlated with the inbound flag_date.
Inbound metadata shows Risk_and_Compliance as destination; outbound department is 'Risk and Compliance', the originating department of the hold notice.
Outbound recipient is 'Accounts' department; no inbound field specifies this downstream consumer.
Fixed enum value 'TRANSACTION_HOLD' with no corresponding inbound field; represents the action type being issued.
Both represent risk severity; inbound enum includes 'HIGH' which maps to outbound 'HIGH' severity.
Inbound account_id (format=account_number) directly corresponds to outbound accountNumber (format=iban).
Identical field name and meaning; unique customer identifier.
Inbound customer_name maps to outbound customerName; same semantic meaning, different casing convention.
Hold reason likely synthesized from risk_category (e.g., 'DEFAULT_RISK'), action_type, and notes to explain the business rationale.
Hold becomes effective around the time the arrears flag is created/updated; both are ISO8601 timestamps.
No inbound field specifies expected hold duration; likely determined by compliance policy or arrears_status category.
Inbound schema contains no transaction-level data; this array is generated downstream by Accounts system.
Transaction-level identifiers not present in inbound arrears flags schema.
Individual transaction amounts not in inbound schema; only account-level outstanding_balance and minimum_payment_due exist.
Currency code not specified in inbound schema; transaction-level detail not available.
Transaction destination country not present in inbound arrears flags data.
Transaction status is outbound-specific; not derived from inbound arrears flags.
Quantified risk score likely computed from severity level and risk category classification.
Risk flags array populated from inbound risk_category and compliance indicators.
Required actions derived from inbound action_type and compliance_action_required flag.
Escalation level computed from severity enum (MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL) mapped to numeric tier.
Approval requirement determined by severity level and compliance action flag.
Specific approver role is a policy/configuration value not present in inbound schema.
Inbound notes field (nullable string) directly maps to outbound notes with same semantic meaning.
Hold expiry date not derivable from inbound arrears flags; determined by compliance policy.
System identifier 'automated_compliance_system_v2.1' is a fixed value, not sourced from inbound data.
Priority 'URGENT' likely assigned when inbound flag_severity is 'CRITICAL' or 'HIGH'.
